new_classes.label_en: Undenatured Ethyl Alcohol of an alcoholic strength of 80 % vol or higher (CN 2207 10 00)
new_classes.label_de: Ethylalkohol mit einem Alkoholgehalt von 80 % vol oder mehr, unvergällt (KN 2207 10 00)
new_classes.definition_en: Ethyl alcohol that has not been denatured and has an actual alcoholic strength by volume of 80 % vol or higher. Spirituous beverages (e.g. gin, vodka), regardless of their alcoholic strength, are excluded and fall under subheadings 2208 20 12 to 2208 90 78.

coverage_explanation: The three core distinguishing criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) the product is undenatured (isDenatured = false via hasValue restriction), (2) the alcoholic strength is ≥ 80 % vol (decimalRange restriction on alcoholByVolumePercent), and (3) spirituous beverages are explicitly excluded via a complement restriction referencing eucn:SpiritousBeverage. A small gap remains: the complement restriction uses a property-filler pattern that approximates the exclusion of spirits but cannot perfectly express "regardless of alcoholic strength" as a full class-level disjointness axiom against SpiritousBeverage within the decimalRange restriction pattern. The HS Explanatory Note cross-reference is purely administrative and carries no additional product-discriminating content. Coverage is therefore rated 0.85.
